task_completion.visual_evidence: PyMOL window is open with the 1GFL structure visible. The object panel on the right should list '1GFL'.

task_completion.description: Select the chromophore (residue 66) and residues within 4.0 Angstroms.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A selection (indicated by pink dots/squares or a named selection in the object panel) is visible around the central chromophore and its immediate surrounding residues.

task_completion.description: Visualize the beta-barrel with transparency or clipping.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main beta-barrel structure is rendered with transparency (e.g., transparent cartoon) or clipping planes are used, making the interior of the barrel visible.

task_completion.description: Prominently display the chromophore and contacting residues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The chromophore and surrounding contacting residues are shown in a distinct, prominent representation (such as sticks or spheres) inside the beta-barrel.

task_completion.description: Save the visualization as a PNG figure.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A PyMOL command (e.g., `png ~/PyMOL_Data/images/gfp_chromophore.png`), a save dialog, or a file manager showing the saved image at the correct path.

task_completion.description: Export a text report of the contacting residues.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor, terminal, or script output showing the creation of `~/PyMOL_Data/gfp_environment_report.txt` with a list of contacting residues (chain, number, name).

integrity.description: Agent interacted with PyMOL to create the visualization.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the PyMOL GUI with the structure being manipulated, not just a script being executed without visual feedback.

integrity.description: The text report contains actual residue data.
integrity.visual_evidence: The report shows specific residue numbers (like 96, 148, 222) and names, not just generic or copy-pasted text from the prompt.

integrity.description: Files are saved to the exact requested paths.
integrity.visual_evidence: Commands or file managers show the exact paths `~/PyMOL_Data/images/gfp_chromophore.png` and `~/PyMOL_Data/gfp_environment_report.txt` being used.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The 1GFL structure does not contain a 'CRO' residue; the chromophore is modeled as standard amino acids, with TYR at position 66. The agent should select residue 66 and its contacts. The text report must be saved to ~/PyMOL_Data/gfp_environment_report.txt and should contain key contacting residues 96, 148, and 222. The figure must be saved to ~/PyMOL_Data/images/gfp_chromophore.png.
